marketFocus.focus: NVIDIA China Sales Stalled, US-Iran Tensions Escalate, Government Shutdown Ended
headlines: NVIDIA AI chip sales to China stalled by US security review—Chinese customers not placing H200 chip orders
US military shot down Iranian surveillance drone near USS Lincoln in Arabian Sea—geopolitical tensions escalate
US House passed (217-214) USD 1.2tln spending package—Trump signed bill to end partial government shutdown
Gold reclaims USD 5,000/oz status (+0.2%), oil remains firm on geopolitical risks (WTI +0.8%, Brent +0.7%)
marketFocus.macroContext: Markets navigate mixed signals: tech sell-off continues (NVIDIA China sales stalled), geopolitical tensions escalate (US-Iran drone incident), but government shutdown ends. APAC stocks ultimately mixed as region partially shrugs off downbeat handover from Wall Street. European equity futures indicate softer cash market open (-0.1%) after Tuesday's -0.2% losses. DXY rangebound after giving back some post-ISM spoils. Treasury futures rangebound amid lack of fresh pertinent drivers. Risk sentiment oscillates between Weak Risk-On and Neutral (67-77 Factors).

tradingSummary: Dollar rangebound (DXY -0.1% at 97.319) after giving back some post-ISM Manufacturing strength—all focus turns to ISM Services PMI (16:00), ADP Employment (14:15), and FOMC Minutes (20:00). USD/JPY +0.4% at 156.31 as safe-haven bid fades. EUR/USD +0.2% at 1.1835, GBP/USD +0.2% at 1.3720. Precious metals reclaim ground: Gold +0.2% approaching USD 5,000/oz, LME Copper -0.8%. Oil remains firm: WTI +0.8%, Brent +0.7% on US-Iran tensions. APAC stocks mixed: Nikkei 225 -0.5%, Hang Seng +0.6%, ASX 200 +0.8%. T-note futures unchanged, 10yr yield 4.278%. Risk sentiment: Weak Risk-On detected (11 Risk ON Factors).

USD.summary: Dollar rangebound after giving back some post-ISM Manufacturing spoils. DXY -0.1% at 97.319, USD/JPY +0.4% at 156.31. US House passed (217-214) USD 1.2tln spending package to fund government—Trump signed bill to end partial government shutdown as expected. US President Trump said they are still negotiating with Iran and that there is more than one meeting with Iran; US military shot down Iranian surveillance drone that approached USS Navy aircraft carrier in Arabian Sea. ADP Employment Change forecast 48K (prev. 41K) at 14:15, ISM Services PMI forecast 53.8 (prev. 54.0) at 16:00, FOMC Minutes at 20:00. 1-3d: Neutral bias, data-dependent, watch ADP/ISM Services/FOMC Minutes.
headlines: US House passed USD 1.2tln spending package—Trump signed to end shutdown
US military shot down Iranian drone near USS Lincoln
ADP Employment forecast 48K (prev. 41K) at 14:15
ISM Services PMI forecast 53.8 (prev. 54.0) at 16:00
FOMC Minutes at 20:00—critical for Fed outlook

EUR.summary: Euro firms modestly—EUR/USD +0.2% at 1.1835. European equity futures indicate softer cash market open (-0.1%) after Tuesday's -0.2% losses. Core Inflation Rate YoY Flash forecast 2.3% (prev. 2.3%), Inflation Rate YoY Flash forecast 1.7% (prev. 1.9%) at 11:00. Final Services PMIs: France forecast 47.9 (prev. 50.1), Germany forecast 53.3 (prev. 52.7), Eurozone forecast 51.9 (prev. 52.4). EUR 4.0bln 2.50% 2032 Bund Auction at 11:30. 1-3d: Neutral bias, inflation data at 11:00 key test, watch Services PMIs.
headlines: Core Inflation Rate YoY Flash forecast 2.3% at 11:00
Inflation Rate YoY Flash forecast 1.7% at 11:00
Final Services PMIs: France 47.9, Germany 53.3, Eurozone 51.9
EUR 4.0bln 2.50% 2032 Bund Auction at 11:30

GBP.summary: Sterling firms modestly—GBP/USD +0.2% at 1.3720. S&P Global Services PMI Final forecast 54.3 (prev. 51.4), Composite PMI Final forecast 53.9 (prev. 51.4) at 10:30. BoE Policy Announcement Thursday 12:00 (forecast 4.75%, prev. 4.75%)—BoE's Bailey Press Conference at 12:30. Earnings from GSK at 13:00. 1-3d: Neutral bias, Services PMI at 10:30 key test, BoE Thursday looms.
headlines: S&P Global Services PMI Final forecast 54.3 at 10:30
BoE Policy Announcement Thursday 12:00—rates on hold expected
BoE's Bailey Press Conference Thursday 12:30
Earnings from GSK at 13:00

JPY.summary: Yen weakens as safe-haven bid fades—USD/JPY +0.4% at 156.31. BoJ won't come to rescue of Takaichi-driven bond rout—sources state Japanese PM Takaichi should not count on BoJ intervention. S&P Global Services PMI Final 53.7 (prev. 51.6), Composite PMI Final 53.1 (prev. 51.1) show services expansion. Chinese RatingDog Composite PMI 51.6 (prev. 51.3), Services PMI 52.3 vs exp. 51.8 (prev. 52.0). Risk-on sentiment returns as APAC stocks partially shrug off tech sell-off. 1-3d: Bearish bias, safe-haven bid fades on risk-on, watch US yields.
headlines: USD/JPY +0.4% at 156.31 as safe-haven bid fades
BoJ won't rescue Takaichi-driven bond rout—sources
S&P Global Services PMI Final 53.7—services expansion
Chinese RatingDog Services PMI 52.3 vs exp. 51.8

AUD.summary: Aussie consolidates post-RBA hawkish hike—AUD/USD rangebound. RBA's Jones to participate at fireside chat at 06:00. AUD 1.2bln 4.25% 2036 AGB Auction at 01:00. S&P Global Composite PMI Final 55.7 (prev. 51.0), Services PMI Final 56.3 (prev. 51.1) show strong services expansion. ASX 200 +0.8% as region partially shrugs off tech sell-off. 1-3d: Neutral bias, post-RBA consolidation, watch RBA's Jones comments at 06:00.

NZD.summary: Kiwi rangebound—NZD/USD around 0.58. Labour Costs Index QoQ 0.4% vs exp. 0.5% (prev. 0.5%), Employment Change QoQ 0.5% vs exp. 0.3% (prev. 0.0%), Unemployment Rate 5.4% vs exp. 5.3% (prev. 5.3%), Participation Rate 70.5% vs exp. 70.3% (prev. 70.3%). Labor market resilient—employment growth beats expectations. 1-3d: Neutral bias, labor market resilient supports NZD.

CAD.summary: Loonie rangebound—USD/CAD around 1.43. Oil remains firm on geopolitical risks: WTI +0.8%, Brent +0.7%. US-Iran tensions escalate after US military shot down Iranian surveillance drone near USS Lincoln. Iranian gunboats approached US oil tanker in Strait of Hormuz early Tuesday—ordered to stop. Weekly Private Crude Stocks (30 Jan, w/e): Crude -11.1mln (exp. +0.5mln), Distillates -4.8mln (exp. -2.3mln), Gasoline +4.7mln. 1-3d: Neutral bias, oil price sensitive, geopolitical tensions support crude.
headlines: WTI +0.8%, Brent +0.7% on US-Iran tensions
US military shot down Iranian drone near USS Lincoln
Weekly Private Crude Stocks -11.1mln vs exp. +0.5mln
Iranian gunboats approached US oil tanker in Strait of Hormuz

CHF.summary: Franc weakens as risk-on sentiment returns—USD/CHF around 0.91. Safe-haven bid fades as APAC stocks partially shrug off tech sell-off. Gold reclaims USD 5,000/oz status (+0.2%) after approaching closer to USD 5,000/oz in early Asia-Pac trade (+1%). APAC stocks ultimately mixed: Nikkei 225 -0.5%, Hang Seng +0.6%, Shanghai Comp +0.6%. 1-3d: Neutral bias, risk-on weighs on CHF, safe-haven bid fades.
